description: Adele's 'Skyfall' is the retained foundation (full mix, own vocal load-bearing). Linkin Park's 'In the End' enters as an added vocal layer and sustains a convincing multi-section overlap from approximately Skyfall chorus 2 through the bridge and final chorus, with a ~22-measure index offset (human-auditioned in a djay Pro session, 2026-07-09, at a 74 BPM shared grid — the witnessed working point of a hypothesized ~74-90 BPM viable region, not a unique correct tempo). The 'hard'-on-'fall' landing is one salient convergence within that window, not the sole target. This record is a WITNESS of a construction family: an existence proof that working arrangements exist here, not a claim that any single parameterization is the unique or best overlay of these recordings.

host.title: Skyfall
host.version_note: Studio single version assumed; a different edit shifts every time value. Pin the exact local file (duration + content hash) in the local annotation file before annotating.

duration_sec.note: measure from the pinned local file

guest.title: In the End
guest.version_note: Studio album version assumed; pin the exact local file before annotating.

duration_sec.note: measure from the pinned local file

host_anchor_section_occurrence.note: the overlap becomes particularly effective from approximately chorus 2 and continues through the bridge and final chorus; exact entry measure unresolved

guest_entry_offset_beats.note: derivable from grid.measure_offset plus the window's precise entry measure once both are resolved

host_bpm.note: Metrical-octave interpretation issue, not an estimation miss: djay initially read Skyfall at ~148 BPM; the user corrected the octave-doubled reading to ~74. The correction changed the available transformation path — it let In the End be SLOWED 105->74 (~-29.5%) instead of accelerated 105->~148 (~+41%, far more drastic). Subject to ordinary verification against the pinned source file (librosa should face the same octave choice). Never copy into a MEASURED TrackAnalysis by hand.

guest_bpm.note: Correctly identified by djay's analyzer in the working session and ratified by ear at the metrical level — a session measurement subject to ordinary re-verification against the pinned source file, NOT an unbounded estimate. Research-layer 'measured' only: this can never flow into a production TrackAnalysis as MEASURED except through the sanctioned measure_tempo seam.

tempo_ratio.note: Guest playback ratio AT THE WITNESSED GRID POINT: 74/105 ~= 0.705 (guest slowed ~29.5%, host essentially unchanged). Grid-choice dependent, and does NOT define the construction: across the hypothesized viable grid region 74-90 BPM the guest ratio runs 0.705-0.857 (at 90: guest -14.3%, host +21.6%). See grid.viable_grid_bpm_region.

guest_key.note: unverified prior (often cited as D#/Eb minor)

pitch_shift_semitones.note: the working session appears to transpose the guest +2 semitones, observed from djay's display. Explicitly flagged for verification from the saved session/source — do not accept the screenshot alone. Whether +2 is part of the successful recipe (vs. incidental) is an open question.

events.lyric_context: 'Let the sky fall' / the sung 'fall' of 'Skyfall'
events.occurrence: each occurrence within the effective window; the intended one is unresolved

time_sec.note: annotate every candidate 'fall' onset across the effective window

events.lyric_context: 'I tried so hard and got so far'
events.occurrence: hook/chorus statement; which repetition lands inside the effective window follows from the measure offset

offset_beats.note: 'hard' lands on 'fall', or within about one host beat of it. Once the corrected beat grids exist, locate both onsets against the grid and check whether the landing is implied by the 22-measure structural offset or requires its own micro-adjustment.

tolerance_beats.note: how far the landing can drift before the effect degrades — to be resolved by the listening pass over the offset grid, not assumed

convergences.note: One salient local convergence WITHIN the extended aligned window — no longer the sole ground-truth target. It may help explain why the broader arrangement feels especially effective; whether it is load-bearing (vs. the window working without it) is an experimental question.

taxonomy_gap_notes: No single MashupMoveType captures this: it is overlay-shaped at macro scale, hook_collision-shaped at the anchor (both vocals audible and load-bearing), genre_contrast in aesthetic, and lyrical_juxtaposition in mechanism (currently out of scope). The host role 'full_mix with load-bearing own vocal' does not exist in TrackRole, and nothing in the production data model encodes entry point, section anchoring, event-level convergence, or a sustained multi-section aligned window. Two further modeling requirements exposed by the session: (1) transformation cost is role-asymmetric — the same percentage tempo change may be acceptable for the conformed guest yet damaging to the host's character, so cost cannot be judged by absolute percentage alone; production tempo scoring anchors on the vocal role but has no host-preservation model. (2) When tempo estimates differ by a metrical octave, candidate generation should compare octave-equivalent interpretations AND the transformation paths/costs they imply before rejecting or heavily penalizing a pairing — the 148-vs-74 reading of the host changed the feasible treatment from ~+41% guest acceleration to ~-29.5% guest slowing. Recorded here as data; see docs/design-memo-skyfall-construction-case.md before proposing any taxonomy change.

shared_grid_bpm.note: the WITNESSED WORKING POINT — the setting both decks were synchronized at in the working audition (2026-07-09) — not the unique or optimal common tempo. Equals the host's corrected metrical tempo, preserving Skyfall at its natural pace. Re-verify from the saved session.

measure_offset.note: host measure = guest measure + 22, read off djay's visible beat grids — which may themselves be mis-gridded. Verify exactness once both grids are corrected against the source audio.

offset_constant_across_window.note: whether the offset holds constant across the entire effective window, or drifts (which would indicate a grid error on one side)

viable_grid_bpm_region.note: PROVISIONAL human-auditioned tempo viability interval, not a verified hard range. The constraint is asymmetric: the host places the main upper bound (beyond some point Skyfall increasingly sounds rushed and loses its intended pacing, weight, and dramatic character), while the guest tolerates substantial slowing from 105. Acceptability is host-character preservation, NOT minimal aggregate transformation — minimizing transformation severity helped FIND this construction but is not a universal selection rule. Exact upper boundary unresolved; resolve via the tempo-sweep audition (see timeline.tempo_auditions).

start_host_measure.note: precise entry measure of the effective overlap

end_host_measure.note: precise exit measure of the effective overlap

judgment.note: human-auditioned 2026-07-09 at offset +22 on the 74 BPM shared grid with the guest apparently at +2 semitones

windows.note: Not merely a single lyric-anchor coincidence: the alignment sustains a convincing multi-section construction over an extended window.

grid.note: Level-2 structural alignment from the djay Pro session. A witnessed construction FAMILY, not a point: the +22 offset with any grid tempo in the viable region is hypothesized workable, different grid tempos may require small alignment adjustments and yield distinct but valid versions, and none of it is the unique valid overlay of these songs.
